league.name: Ligue 1
league.description: France's top division mixes Paris Saint-Germain's modern star power with historic clubs from Marseille, Lyon, Monaco, Lille, and other strong regional football cities.
league.styleOfPlay: Athletic, fast, and development-heavy. Ligue 1 often gives young players real minutes, while top clubs blend direct transitions with technical attacking midfielders and quick wide players.
famousClubs: Paris Saint-Germain — Paris superclub and modern title force
Marseille — southern giant with European pedigree
Monaco — talent factory from the principality
Lille — northern club with recent title success
league.historySummary: French football has produced waves of elite talent, from Marseille's European triumph to Lyon's domestic dynasty and PSG's Qatar-backed era. The league is often watched for the next breakout star as much as for the title race.
rivalries: Choc des Olympiques — Lyon vs Marseille
Derby du Nord — Lille vs Lens
league.fanGuide: Start with Le Classique to understand the emotional map: Paris against Marseille. Then follow Lyon and Monaco for academy and talent-development storylines.

teams.name: Paris Saint-Germain
teams.shortHistory: PSG grew from a young Paris club into France's dominant modern force, with the Qatari ownership era turning the Parc des Princes into a global football stage.
teams.fanGuide: Dark blue, red, and Paris identity. Le Classique against Marseille is the fixture to learn first; European expectations shape every season.
legends: Zlatan Ibrahimovic — striker who defined early QSI dominance

teams.name: Marseille
teams.shortHistory: Marseille are France's southern giant and the first French club to win the European Cup. Their support, noise, and suspicion of Paris drive much of Ligue 1's emotion.
teams.fanGuide: White and sky blue; the Velodrome is the sound. Le Classique vs PSG is about geography, culture, and power as much as football.
legends: Didier Drogba — explosive striker and cult hero
Basila Boli — 1993 European Cup final scorer

teams.name: Lyon
teams.shortHistory: Lyon dominated France in the 2000s and built one of Europe's most respected academy pipelines, producing technical midfielders and forwards for bigger stages.
teams.fanGuide: White kits with red and blue; academy pride matters. The derby against Saint-Etienne is the local grudge, while Marseille games carry national weight.

teams.name: Monaco
teams.shortHistory: Monaco play in the French system from the principality and are known for waves of elite talent, from European finals to the Mbappe-led title team of 2017.
teams.fanGuide: Red and white diagonal shirts; smaller stadium, big talent reputation. Watch Monaco to spot future stars before they move.

teams.name: Lille
teams.shortHistory: Lille are a northern club with a modern recruitment reputation and a famous 2021 title that interrupted PSG's dominance.
teams.fanGuide: Red, navy, and northern pride. The Derby du Nord against Lens is the fixture that explains local identity.

teams.name: RC Lens
teams.shortHistory: Mining-town club from northern France that won their only league title in 1998. Lens represent a working-class football tradition in the Nord-Pas-de-Calais region, and their return to the top flight brought passionate support back to Bollaert.
teams.fanGuide: The Derby du Nord against Lille is the region's defining rivalry. Bollaert's steep terraces create one of French football's noisiest atmospheres.

teams.name: OGC Nice
teams.shortHistory: Côte d'Azur club who won four French championships in the 1950s. INEOS ownership has brought resources and ambition to a club with one of French football's most scenic stadium locations. Nice under Lucien Favre developed a reputation for solid defensive organization.
teams.fanGuide: Nice football blends Mediterranean style with new-money ambition. The Allianz Riviera opened in 2013 and offers views across the hills above the Côte d'Azur.

teams.name: Stade Rennais
teams.shortHistory: Brittany's biggest club, Rennes have become a genuine Ligue 1 force and regular Europa League participants. Their academy is one of French football's most productive, with Eduardo Camavinga among recent graduates.
teams.fanGuide: The Grand Ouest derby against Nantes is Brittany versus Loire. Roazhon Park — 'Roazhon' being the Breton name for Rennes — reflects the club's regional pride.

teams.name: RC Strasbourg
teams.shortHistory: Alsatian club that won the 1979 French championship and have a long history reflecting the cross-border culture of the Alsace region. Chelsea owner Todd Boehly acquired a stake in 2022, bringing new investment to the club.
teams.fanGuide: Strasbourg's Alsatian identity — between France and Germany — gives the club a unique cultural flavour. The Meinau ground is compact and atmospheric.

players.name: Abdallah Sima
players.quickFact: Senegal winger whose pace and direct dribbling made him a Lens outlet on the counter after spells in Portugal and England.
players.playingStyle: Explosive wide forward who runs in behind, takes on defenders, and finishes with power on transitions.
quizHints: Senegal international wide player known for speed on the break.
Direct winger who attacks space and drives at full-backs.
Attacker whose first name is Abdallah and surname is Sima.
